# bithuman

> First-party command-line tool for the bitHuman avatar platform, distributed
> via this Homebrew tap and the universal installer. Run a live, talking avatar
> in your browser, render avatars to MP4 offline, manage showcase models, and
> drive the whole platform from an AI agent. Built for both humans and AI
> agents: every command supports `--json` with a stable, versioned schema and
> sysexits exit codes, and a built-in MCP server (`bithuman mcp`).

For agents: pass `--json` to any command for one machine-readable object on
stdout; failures emit `{"error":{"code","message","command"}}` on stderr and a
typed exit code (0 ok, 2 usage, 66 not-found, 69 unavailable, 70 internal, 77
auth). stdout is data only. Authenticate headless with `BITHUMAN_API_SECRET`,
or `printf %s "$KEY" | bithuman login --with-token`. Full contract:
https://docs.bithuman.ai/cli/agents

## Install
- macOS (Apple Silicon): `brew install bithuman`
- macOS + Linux (x86_64/aarch64): `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bithuman/main/install.sh | sh`
- pip (macOS arm64): `pip install bithuman-cli`

The conversation brain is included with your account — `bithuman run`
bootstraps a managed brain on first use (billed to credits; just `bithuman
login`). No `pip install` needed for chat.

## Self-describing entrypoints (no docs needed)
- `bithuman __schema`: entire command/flag/exit-code tree + MCP tool catalog as
  JSON, generated from the installed binary — the source of truth, never drifts.
- `bithuman <command> --help`: per-command flags + a copy-pasteable `EXAMPLES:` block.
- `bithuman completion bash|zsh|fish|elvish|powershell`: shell completions.

## Common commands
- `bithuman run <slug-or-path>`: live, talking avatar in the browser. `--json` emits a `session_started` event with the URL.
- `bithuman doctor --json`: install health; exit 0 iff `.ready` is true.
- `bithuman list --json`: showcase manifest; `.models[].slug` are downloadable.
- `bithuman pull <slug>`: download a model; prints the cached `.imx` path.
- `bithuman info <model.imx> --json`: model metadata.
- `bithuman render <model.imx> -a in.wav -o out.mp4`: offline MP4 (Linux-only encoder).
- `bithuman whoami --json` / `bithuman account --json`: identity, plan, credits.
- `bithuman mcp`: built-in MCP server over stdio (JSON-RPC) for MCP clients —
  cloud tools (TTS, agents, dynamics, embed, webhooks, credits) + local tools.
